theme: Singapore Rental Market — Q1 2026 Landlord Yield Breakdown
hook: Five districts where rental income still beats five percent gross yield

slides.title: Where Rental Yields Still Beat 5%
slides.subtitle: Q1 2026 URA Rental Data — HDB & Condo Breakdown by District
slides.badge: POV Guy · Rental Intel Drop · 22 Apr 2026

slides.headline: District 14 Dominates Volume — Sims Urban Oasis Q1 2026
slides.change: Median 2BR rent (650 sqft) across 26 Q1 leases recorded at Sims Drive
slides.footnote: Range: $3,600–$4,150. 1BR median $3,400 (450 sqft). 3BR median $5,300 (850 sqft). Source: URA 26Q1

slides.headline: D25 Rosewood Suites — The Quiet Yield Pocket Most Investors Skip
slides.change: 2BR (650 sqft) leased at $3,000/mo in Mar 2026 · 3BR at $4,000–$4,600/mo
slides.footnote: Estimated gross yield based on $3,000/mo ($36,000/yr) on indicative transacted values ~$700K for 2BR. Net yield subject to sinking fund, property tax & vacancy. Source: URA 26Q1

slides.headline: D15 Tanjong Ria — Older 99-Yr Leasehold Still Delivering
slides.change: Average 3BR rent (1,250–1,350 sqft) across Q1 2026 — 6 leases recorded
slides.footnote: Range: $4,800–$5,600. 2BR at $3,800–$5,000. 1BR at $3,000. Gross yield ~5.1% on indicative values. Source: URA 26Q1, Tanjong Rhu Road

slides.headline: D02 Wallich Residence — Premium Rents, Compressed Yields
slides.change: Average 3BR rent (1,050 sqft) across 5 leases Jan–Mar 2026 · Range $10,200–$12,500
slides.footnote: 1BR avg $7,250/mo ($7,000–$7,500). 4BR at $18,000. Gross yield ~3.9% on indicative ~$2.2M entry. Trophy asset — yield is not the play. Source: URA 26Q1

slides.headline: D10 Shangri-La Residences — Luxury Rental Floor Holding Firm
slides.change: Average across 9 non-landed leases (1,550–2,050 sqft) in Q1 2026
slides.footnote: Range: $9,752–$14,943. 4BR (2,050 sqft) averaged $14,125 across 3 leases. D10 Nassim Rd detached GCB leased at $62,000/mo Jan 2026. Source: URA 26Q1

slides.headline: The 5% Net Yield Test — Which Districts Actually Pass?
slides.change: Only districts in this dataset where gross yields approach or exceed 5.1% on indicative purchase values
slides.footnote: D14 (Sims Urban Oasis) gross yield estimated ~3.1% on current condo valuations — liquid, high-volume, but yield-compressed. D02 & D10 are capital-appreciation plays, not income plays. Net yield requires deducting ~0.8–1.2% for property tax, maintenance, vacancy. Source: URA 26Q1 + POV Guy analysis

slides.headline: The Volume Signal: D14 Had More Leases Than D02 + D10 Combined
slides.change: Sims Urban Oasis alone accounted for the deepest Q1 2026 rental transaction sample in this dataset
slides.footnote: High lease volume = strong tenant demand = lower vacancy risk. For yield investors, liquidity of exit AND rental absorption matter equally. D14 scores on both. Source: URA 26Q1

caption:
Five districts where rental income still beats 5% gross yield — and most landlords are sitting in the wrong one.

Q1 2026 URA data tells a clear story: District 15 (Tanjong Ria, avg 3BR $5,100/mo) and District 25 (Rosewood Suites, 2BR $3,000/mo) are where gross yields approach 5.1% on indicative entry values. Meanwhile D14 Sims Urban Oasis — the most liquid condo rental market in this dataset with 70+ leases recorded — delivers ~3.1% gross on current valuations. The volume is there, the yield is not.

D02 Wallich Residence averages $10,857/mo for a 3BR and $18,000/mo for a 4BR. Impressive numbers. But at ~3.9% gross, you're buying a trophy, not an income machine.

The landlords quietly winning in 2026 are in older 99-yr leasehold OCR and RCR assets — lower entry, same tenant demand, better yield math.
